# WordPress Tips  You’ll  Wish You Knew Sooner

## Metadata
- **From:** Syed Balkhi - WPBeginner <support@wpbeginner.com>
- **Date:** 2025-09-13 15:00:00
- **Source:** Gmail IMAP
- **UID:** 1084

## Body
Hey Muhammad,

Last week, I shared with you how to customize your site’s look
and functionality using code snippets.

Several of you asked me what happens if you ever want to revert a
change or tweak a snippet you’ve already added.

Maybe you made a few small edits, then realized things looked
better before… and wanted to roll them back.

The problem is, when you edit your theme files directly, there’s
no version history to fall back on. Even worse, a tiny change can
sometimes lead to unexpected issues across your site.

This is why I always recommend adding code snippets in WordPress
the right way.

How to properly add code snippets in WordPress 
( https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9._X19zPTBhNjJ4bzR6ZGdjZ3ViMDZydGpjIn0.wgdCCa06EzOekdVHVpgjq8t2tcVHt4fM3pBsIlNkals )
 (the right way).

The best part is that it gives you the flexibility to view,
compare, and restore previous versions of your snippets with
ease—so you’re always in control.

You might also want to take a look at 40+ extremely useful  
( https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..nbkvZiOJW9fRFNMHFf8l5V4q4ciftvwsIonIFAFgiiI )
WordPress tricks for your functions.php file.

-------------------------------------------

What if you don’t just want to tweak your site but build an
entire one from scratch?

---------------------------

feel like an endless project.

tweaking colors, layouts, and menus… only to feel stuck with
something that’s still “not quite right.”
